title: blechnum coriaceum
intro: Blechnum coriaceum, also known as the rough-woolly fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Blechnum coriaceum has been found to possess medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an anti-oxidant and anti-cancer agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infused in water, with some cultures also using it in combination with other herbs to create tonics and remedies.
